title: Rocket League®

notes: Runs good out of the box. (and better than native version)
title: Rocket League®

notes: Works better than native except for minor slowdowns in menu initially connecting online and when selecting to connect to an online game, otherwise perfect.
title: Rocket League®

notes: Forcing Steam Play/Proton because the Linux build has a bug with the Steam Controller support where the controller will not work in-game. Menus work fine, and changing to generic xinput controls work fine, but I want to use the native SIAPI support which works fine on the Windows build of the game. Steam Controller works perfectly with Rocket League using Steam Play/Proton.
title: Rocket League®

notes: Proton/Wine version better than native - xbox Rumble works. But bad split-screen performance...
title: Rocket League®

notes:
Native has some visual and audio problems for me. White triangle artifacts, ambient occlusion producing rainbow colored static, sound not playing, sounds playing too many times, alt-tabbing ruining the "full-screen" window position (this is probably a GNOME issue). 

So I tried running it with Proton. Proton only problem is that it stutters in menus, sometimes when the match starts or when a goal is scored. (feels like asset loading) 
When launching the game it takes 10 seconds for the season pass to show in the menu, it takes another 20 seconds for the news tab to show up, and another 25 seconds for the images in the news tab to display. 

The stuttering is probably also caused by this slow loading. 
In the native version everything loads instantly.
title: Rocket League®

notes:
Works better than the native version.
There were two issues on the native version: the controller vibrations didn't work and the loading screen took a while to load the background.
The proton version fixes these bugs. The controller vibrations sometimes doesn't work though. One thing to notice is that, for me, at least, the "Play Online" window sometimes lags when opened (that did not happen on the native version).

Overall, work flawlessly. It's not exactly the same but it does not affect the gameplay at all. Have fun playing Rocket League :)
title: Rocket League®

gpu: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2080 with Max-Q Design
notes: Runs better(!) with Proton than it does its native Linux version. Native Linux version was missing AA and mouse input. Works flawlessly out of the box with Proton.
title: Rocket League®

notes: Game works great out of the box on Proton. As this works via DX9, I installed manually D9VK and it worked even via this Vulkan translation surprisingly well.
title: Rocket League®

notes:
I played the native version and with Proton.
The native version was worse than Proton.
The controller worked for me and with Proton, RL worked out of the box with no issues.
title: Rocket League®

notes:
The Windows version of the game runs well through SteamPlay/Proton.
I tried installing D9VK since the game runs on DirectX 9. The performance was way better, but the game crashes sometimes after installing D9VK. It rarely crashed even before installing D9VK but it might have not been a Proton issue. I also tried running the game via regular Wine with D9VK installed and I didn't experience any crashes.
title: Rocket League®

notes: Better than the native version and runs almost as well as in windows
title: Rocket League®

notes: Runs better than native version with latest Proton update. Improvements over native version: no audio glitches anymore, controller rumble works and you can theoretically use RL mods with the Windows version. Runs perfectly fine in 4K!
title: Rocket League®

notes: The game runs better with Proton than with the native Linux support, I just had to downscale a bit the game compared to Windows but I managed to get 60 FPS with everything on High. Working perfectly!
title: Rocket League®

notes: Works better than native.  D9VK performance is better than WineD3D, but more prone to crashes.  Steam Controller's rumble does not work in either Proton or Native.
title: Rocket League®

notes: For some reason sound in native Linux game is not working. I had to switch to proton in order to have sound.
title: Rocket League®

notes: Everything works as expected...
title: Rocket League®

notes: Works much better with Proton. I get probably about +50% FPS using Proton vs Linux Native. Controller rumble also works with proton, and doesn't with native. Would definitely recommend using proton. Didn't have to tweak any settings.
title: Rocket League®

notes: I had problems with the native version, couldn't use my xbox 360 controller. Worked amazing with proton 4.2

notes: Play with D9VK: PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command%
title: Rocket League®

notes: On Intel HD 4000 i5 3320m the game runs stable after locking the FPS rate to 36 ...... It is stable at 720p, otherwise the FPS gets higher but with annoying drops
title: Rocket League®

notes: Works perefectly out of the box without any tuning. Got over 120 fps at 2560x1440 at highest settings, much more than the native version.
title: Rocket League®

notes: Played about 3 hours with Proton and D9VK. It crashed twice in that time. I hope it gets more stable soon, because the performance was great. For a stable frame rate I played the native version at 1080p 120fps, with D9VK I can play at 1440p 144fps.
title: Rocket League®

notes:
Game runs fine when native mode is used, however with Proton I have noticed that it runs even better, there is no stuttering or FPS drops. 
However with Plasma KDE(unsure about other Desktop Environments) if game is set to Borderless, the task bar will overlap the game, pressing Alt+F3 then going into 'More Actions' and choosing Fullscreen solves this minor issue.
title: Rocket League®

notes: I did leave review like two weeks ago but I forgot to mention one thing, when running as native, I noticed memory leaks at first memory usage is about 1.6GBs but after playing it skyrockets to 5GB of RAM. Switching to Proton solved this completely and memory usage stays at about 1.3GB of RAM. If you do notice memory leaks, just run game through proton and you will be good to go.
title: Rocket League®

notes:
I am only using the proton version as i carnt for the life of me fix my controller on the native game. After adding "PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command%" to launch options i was able to get the same performance as the native version or windows version. 

The only slight negative to running RL like this is that it seems to have a fractionally higher input lag than on windows but this is just based of feeling not off of actual measurement of the input lag.
title: Rocket League®

notes: Runs better than native version.
title: Rocket League®

notes: I use PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command% in recommendation from other reports just in case. Been playing for hours and no performance issues at all. I experienced a crash once in 4 hours of continuous gameplay. Also working perfect with my Wooting keyboard. Perfect!
title: Rocket League®
